techniques.evidence_quote: there was a misconfiguration in S3, leading to unauthorized access to the Bucket, allowing attackers to list, read, or write to the S3 bucket at will
JS resource files hosted on S3 (AWS's object storage service) were compromised and tampered with by hackers
injecting malicious JS scripts. When users performed regular operations, the malicious code was executed

techniques.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
techniques.evidence_quote: the JS resource files hosted on S3 (AWS's object storage service) were compromised and tampered with by hackers
All C-end interactions of the attack occurred on the official Premint website
replacing the original verification signature with a transaction that authorized the attacker to transfer users' high-value NFTs
Once this transaction was signed, the assets would be stolen

attack_chain.description: Attacker discovered a misconfigured S3 bucket (s3-redwood-labs.premint.xyz) belonging to Premint with overly permissive access
attack_chain.evidence_quote: we suspect that there was a misconfiguration in S3, leading to unauthorized access to the Bucket

evidence_chunks.quote: we suspect that there was a misconfiguration in S3, leading to unauthorized access to the Bucket
we suspect that there was a misconfiguration in S3, leading to unauthorized access to the Bucket

attack_chain.description: Exploited S3 bucket misconfiguration (global write access) to gain unauthorized read/write access to the bucket hosting Premint's JS assets
attack_chain.evidence_quote: allowing attackers to list, read, or write to the S3 bucket at will, thus tampering with the JS resource files

evidence_chunks.quote: allowing attackers to list, read, or write to the S3 bucket at will, thus tampering with the JS resource files
allowing attackers to list, read, or write to the S3 bucket at will, thus tampering with the JS resource files

attack_chain.description: Tampered with boomerang.min.js hosted in the S3 bucket, injecting code that loads a malicious script from an attacker-controlled lookalike domain (s3-redwood-labs-premint-xyz.com)
attack_chain.evidence_quote: This file was injected by hackers with a script that loads another attack script file hosted on a fake domain owned by the hackers

evidence_chunks.quote: This file was injected by hackers with a script that loads another attack script file hosted on a fake domain owned by the hackers
This file was injected by hackers with a script that loads another attack script file hosted on a fake domain owned by the hackers

attack_chain.description: When users visited premint.xyz, the compromised boomerang.min.js pulled and executed the attacker's cdn.min.js in the user's browser
Malicious script replaced the legitimate 'Verify wallet ownership' signature request with a setApprovalForAll transaction granting attacker approval over users' NFTs
attack_chain.evidence_quote: replacing the original verification signature with a transaction that authorized the attacker to transfer users' high-value NFTs

evidence_chunks.quote: replacing the original verification signature with a transaction that authorized the attacker to transfer users' high-value NFTs
replacing the original verification signature with a transaction that authorized the attacker to transfer users' high-value NFTs

attack_chain.description: After users signed the malicious setApprovalForAll authorization, the attacker transferred out their high-value NFTs
attack_chain.evidence_quote: After obtaining the authorization, the attacker stole users' NFTs and other assets

evidence_chunks.quote: After obtaining the authorization, the attacker stole users' NFTs and other assets
After obtaining the authorization, the attacker stole users' NFTs and other assets

initial_access_vector: Misconfigured S3 bucket with global/unauthorized write access allowed attackers to tamper with JS files (boomerang.min.js) served by premint.xyz
persistence_mechanism: Malicious JS remained in the compromised S3-hosted boomerang.min.js for ~6 hours after discovery; tampered file continued to be served from the bucket
privilege_escalation: None described; S3 misconfiguration directly granted write access needed to tamper with assets
